mission.title: Install and Configure Prometheus Operator / kube-prometheus-stack
mission.description: The Prometheus Operator simplifies the deployment and management of Prometheus monitoring stacks on Kubernetes using custom resources. It is ideal for users looking to automate monitoring configurations and manage Prometheus instances seamlessly. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes custom resources and potential complexity in advanced configurations.

steps.title: Add the Prometheus Operator Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Prometheus Operator Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add prometheus-community https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This is the official repository for the Prometheus Operator.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Prometheus Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Prometheus Operator along with its Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) in the specified namespace.

```bash

helm install prometheus-operator prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack --namespace monitoring --create-namespace --version 0.90.1

```

> This installs the kube-prometheus-stack with the specified version.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check the status of the Prometheus Operator and its components to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n monitoring

kubectl get svc -n monitoring

```

> This command lists all pods in the monitoring namespace.
> This command lists all services in the monitoring namespace.

steps.title: Create a Prometheus custom resource
steps.description:
This step creates a basic Prometheus instance using a custom resource definition.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1
kind: Prometheus
metadata:
  name: example-prometheus
  namespace: monitoring
spec:
  replicas: 1
  serviceAccountName: prometheus-operator
  serviceMonitorSelector: {}
EOF

```

> This creates a simple Prometheus instance.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1
kind: Prometheus
metadata:
  name: example-prometheus
  namespace: monitoring
spec:
  replicas: 1
  serviceAccountName: prometheus-operator
  serviceMonitorSelector: {}
EOF

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Prometheus Operator
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Prometheus Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all monitoring data and configurations.

```bash

helm uninstall prometheus-operator --namespace monitoring

kubectl delete namespace monitoring

```

> This command uninstalls the Prometheus Operator from the monitoring namespace.
> This command deletes the monitoring namespace and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Prometheus Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations and data. This process involves upgrading the Helm release and verifying the installation.

```bash

kubectl get prometheus -n monitoring -o yaml > prometheus-backup.yaml

helm upgrade prometheus-operator prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack --namespace monitoring --version 0.90.1

kubectl get pods -n monitoring

```

> Backup the current Prometheus configuration.
> This command upgrades the Prometheus Operator to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get prometheus -n monitoring -o yaml > prometheus-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error creating: pods "prometheus-operator-..." is forbidden: error looking up service account monitoring/prometheus-operator: serviceaccount "prometheus-operator" not found
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service account required for the Prometheus Operator is missing.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service account is created or specify an existing one in the Prometheus custom resource.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image "docker.io/prometheus/prometheus:v2.30.0": rpc error: code = NotFound desc = manifest for docker.io/prometheus/prometheus:v2.30.0 not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified Prometheus image version does not exist.

**Fix:**
Check the available image versions and update the Prometheus custom resource accordingly.

troubleshooting.title: Prometheus is not scraping targets.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service monitor may not be configured correctly or the labels do not match.

**Fix:**
Verify the service monitor configuration and ensure the labels match those defined in the Prometheus custom resource.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl apply -f prometheus-backup.yaml' fails with 'the server could not find the requested resource'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The API version in the backup file may be outdated or incorrect.

**Fix:**
Update the API version in the backup file to match the current CRD version.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Prometheus features including enhanced alerting rules and improved Grafana dashboards.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of legacy service monitors in favor of the new service monitor API.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.75 or v0.76, review the new service monitor API documentation and update your configurations accordingly.

resolution.summary: You should see the Prometheus instance running with the command 'kubectl get prometheus -n monitoring' showing the status as 'Running'.
codeSnippets:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1
kind: Prometheus
metadata:
  name: example-prometheus
  namespace: monitoring
spec:
  replicas: 1
  serviceAccountName: prometheus-operator
  serviceMonitorSelector: {}
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required resources and the necessary tools installed.
